title: Create NetworkingOnboarding 4-step guided tour for first-time visitors
description: The existing 5-step tour is System Design-only (references component-palette, canvas nodes, status-bar). Networking has ZERO onboarding — zero tooltips, zero guided tour, zero first-visit detection. A complete beginner scores 2/10 on first 60 seconds. Create a 4-step tour: (1) 'Welcome to Networking' overview, (2) 'Step Through Protocols' spotlighting Step button, (3) 'Watch the Diagram' spotlighting sequence diagram, (4) 'Explore More Protocols' spotlighting protocol list. Use per-module first-visit detection in localStorage.
acceptanceCriteria: 4-step guided tour fires on first Networking module visit
Tour uses the existing OnboardingOverlay spotlight mask system
Step 2 spotlights the Step button with 'Click to advance one message at a time'
Tour can be skipped and replayed from settings
Tour works on mobile viewport
Per-module localStorage key (architex-networking-onboarding-done)

title: Auto-open bottom panel on first Networking visit so step descriptions are visible
description: Bottom panel is closed by default (ui-store.ts bottomPanelOpen: false). The bottom panel contains the ONLY step-by-step descriptions explaining what each protocol message means and why. A new user who never discovers Cmd+J will miss all educational content. Fix: on first Networking visit, set bottomPanelOpen: true. Use per-module localStorage flag.
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom panel auto-opens on first Networking module visit
Subsequent visits respect user's last panel state
Step descriptions visible immediately for first-time users
Does not interfere with other modules' panel state

title: Add tooltips to Step, Play All, Reset buttons and all protocol-specific controls
description: Zero tooltips exist anywhere in the 2814-line NetworkingModule.tsx. 'Step' does what? 'Play All' plays what? Controls like TLS 1.2 toggle, DNS scenario selector, CORS inputs, and serialization editor have no explanatory text. Fix: add tooltips using the existing tooltip component or title attributes: Step='Advance to the next protocol message', Play All='Auto-play all remaining steps at 600ms intervals', Reset='Return to the first step'. Add tooltips to TLS toggles, DNS scenarios, CORS inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Step, Play All, Reset buttons all have descriptive tooltips
All 9 protocol buttons have tooltips with brief description
TLS toggles have tooltips explaining comparison and 0-RTT modes
CORS inputs have tooltips explaining each field's purpose
Tooltips accessible via keyboard focus (not just hover)

title: Add completion state when all steps are exhausted — celebration + next protocol suggestion
description: When stepIndex reaches totalSteps-1, Step and Play buttons grey out with no feedback. The user is at a dead end with no 'you did it!' or 'try this next'. Fix: show a completion card replacing the step description: (1) '🎉 You've completed the TCP Handshake!' (2) Brief 1-sentence summary of what was learned, (3) 'Next: Try TLS 1.3' button linking to the next protocol. Add subtle confetti or checkmark animation.
acceptanceCriteria: Completion card appears when all steps are viewed
Card shows protocol name, brief summary, and 'Next Protocol' button
Next protocol suggestion is context-aware (TCP→TLS→DNS progression)
Subtle visual celebration (checkmark animation, not overwhelming)
Card dismissed when user switches protocols or resets

title: Add 30-second idle nudge for first-time Networking visitors
description: Zero stuck detection exists. If a user sits on the page for 30 seconds without clicking, nothing happens. Fix: for first-time visitors (not returning users), if no interaction detected in 30s, show a subtle pulsing tooltip on the Step button: 'Click here to see the first message in the TCP handshake →'. Dismiss after first click. Only fires once per session.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle nudge appears after 30s of no interaction on first visit
Nudge spotlights the Step button with actionable text
Nudge dismisses on any user interaction
Does not fire for returning users (localStorage flag)
Does not fire if user has already clicked Step

title: Add 'What is this protocol?' beginner primer before step 1 for each protocol
description: When a beginner selects TCP Handshake, they see 'SYN seq=0 ack=0' immediately — meaningless to someone who doesn't know TCP. Fix: for each protocol, add a collapsible 2-sentence primer at the top of the properties panel: TCP='Every time you load a webpage, your computer and the server verify they can communicate. This 3-step handshake ensures both sides are ready before any data flows.' Show by default for first-time users, collapsible for returning users.
acceptanceCriteria: Each of 9 protocols has a 2-sentence plain-English primer
Primer appears at the top of the properties panel
Collapsible with 'Show less' toggle
Uses beginner-friendly language (no jargon in the primer itself)
First-time users see it expanded; returning users see it collapsed

title: Add 'Start Here' badge and recommended first protocol for new users
description: 9 protocols listed flat with no indication of where to start. A complete beginner has analysis paralysis. Fix: add a '⭐ Start Here' badge on TCP Handshake (the simplest and most foundational protocol). Add a subtle dotted border or background highlight on the recommended first protocol. Badge only shows on first visit.
acceptanceCriteria: TCP Handshake has a 'Start Here' badge on first visit
Badge is visually distinct but not overwhelming (subtle star icon + text)
Badge disappears after user completes TCP handshake steps
Other protocols have difficulty indicators (beginner/intermediate/advanced)

title: Make onboarding system per-module instead of platform-wide one-shot
description: OnboardingOverlay fires once platform-wide (architex_onboarding_completed localStorage). Once dismissed, NO module ever gets an introduction again. Fix: refactor to per-module first-visit detection. Each module can define its own tour steps. The activity bar tracks which modules have been 'introduced'. A first visit to any new module can trigger that module's specific onboarding.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-module localStorage keys: architex-{module}-onboarded
Each module can register its own OnboardingStepDef[] array
First visit to a new module triggers that module's tour
Returning to an already-introduced module skips the tour
Settings panel has 'Replay Tour' per module
